description: In Java/JVM, the JDK version, bytecode target, and JIT/GC configuration are part of the contract and changing them changes behavior.
body: In Java/JVM, the JDK version, bytecode target, and JIT/GC configuration define how code compiles, lints, and resolves dependencies; bumping any of them can change diagnostics and emitted output. Use when you touch the JDK version, bytecode target, and JIT/GC configuration or when a build behaves differently than the pinned environment. Do not silently upgrade the toolchain or override the pinned version to make an error disappear. Actions: read the pinned config, match the project's the JDK version, bytecode target, and JIT/GC configuration, then run SpotBugs, Error Prone, and Checkstyle reports and the Maven/Gradle module task (mvn -pl / gradle :module:test) with the right JDK toolchain to compare against the baseline. Validation signals include identical SpotBugs, Error Prone, and Checkstyle reports output, a reproducible build, and unchanged lockfile resolution. Risk boundary: a toolchain bump is a deliberate, reviewed change; escalate rather than upgrading to unblock yourself.
